ddls:
CREATE TABLE "clothing_items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clothing item */,
  "item_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing item (e.g., pants, shorts, skirt) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the clothing item */,
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the brand the item belongs to */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the item belongs to */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL for an image of the clothing item */,
  "is_customizable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the item supports custom size charts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "length_measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each length measurement */,
  "chart_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size chart the length measurement belongs to */,
  "length_type" TEXT /* Type of length measurement (e.g., 30", 32", 34") */,
  "length" TEXT /* Length measurement value (e.g., 76cm) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thigh_measurements" (
  "thigh_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each thigh measurement */,
  "chart_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size chart the thigh measurement belongs to */,
  "thigh_measurement" TEXT /* Thigh measurement value (e.g., 54cm / 21.2") */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("thigh_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rise_measurements" (
  "rise_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rise measurement */,
  "chart_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size chart the rise measurement belongs to */,
  "rise_measurement" TEXT /* Rise measurement value (e.g., 52cm / 20.5") */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rise_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "preferred_size" TEXT /* Preferred size of the customer for quick reference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the order */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clothing item ordered */,
  "size" TEXT /* Size of the clothing item ordered */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address for the order */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "total_amount" TEXT /* Total amount of the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES clothing_items ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, product manager, customer service) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
